title: Business Analyst (m/w/d) ServiceNow für ITSM
required_skills: Degree in Business Informatics, Computer Science, or Business with IT focus
Several years experience as Business Analyst in ITSM/ServiceNow environment
In-depth ITIL knowledge and experience in Incident, Request, Problem, Change, Knowledge management
ServiceNow expertise: Servicekatalog, Flow Designer, Performance Analytics
Requirements documentation: User Stories, acceptance criteria, process modelling (BPMN 2.0, UML)
Experience with test planning and UAT execution
bonus_skills: Experience working with external software vendors
matched_skills: Agile methodology experience — sprint planning, stand-ups, retrospectives at Hapag-Lloyd
Documentation support and process facilitation (Hapag-Lloyd, HSBC)
missing_skills: No BPMN 2.0 or UML process modelling
No User Story writing or acceptance criteria formulation experience
No UAT planning or execution experience
German fluency at C1 required — candidate has B1
core_responsibilities: Analyse and structure requirements from IT and non-IT departments, translate into ServiceNow-native solutions
Write User Stories, Use Cases, and acceptance criteria with Product Owner and dev team
Create process models for ITSM workflows (Incident, Service Request, Problem, Change, Knowledge) in BPMN/UML
Plan and conduct tests, UAT scenarios through to go-live
Document KPIs, SLAs, and dashboard requirements
Accompany changes, releases, upgrades with documentation and training
recommended_emphasis: Do not apply — ServiceNow/ITIL is a hard technical requirement with no transferable equivalent in the candidate's background
German at B1 is insufficient for a role requiring 'sehr gut Deutsch'
The agile sprint experience at Hapag-Lloyd is the only tangential overlap but does not substitute for ITSM domain expertise
summary: Match score is 9.5 (poor_match). This role requires deep ServiceNow platform expertise, ITIL certification, and ITSM domain knowledge — skills the candidate entirely lacks. Additionally, the role demands near-native German proficiency that the candidate's B1 level cannot meet. The only overlap is agile methodology participation, which is insufficient. This is a clear non-fit and should not be applied to.
